title: glechoma italica
intro: Glechoma italica, also known as Creeping Thyme or Woolly Mint, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Glechoma italica include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and even exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Glechoma italica can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is also used as an ingredient in traditional herbal remedies.
